items.note: This gemstone is a polished pink sphere. When viewed at a specific angle with a source of light an asterism (star-effect) is visible across it's surface. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Steel Spiked Gauntlets
items.note: A pair of blue enameled steel spiked gauntlets. The palms of both gauntlets are designed to display the Cambersome family crest, a stylized ‘C’ shaped into the likeness of a coiled serpent.

items.name: Studded Leather Armor
items.note:
Made from tough but flexible leather, studded leather is reinforced with close-set rivets or spikes. | Light Armor
Armor Class: 12 + Dex Modifier (Source: SRD)

items.name: Stuffed Falcon
items.note: A stuffed falcon, tattered with age, with black onyx eyes. (Onyx value: 5gp each)

items.name: Sunrod Quiver
items.note: A short quiver holding 3 sunrods.

items.name: Sunstone Gem
items.note: Sunstone is a feldspar jem, closely related to moonstone, and more properly known as oligoclase. Sunstone can be colorless or faintly greenish and of facet grade, but most common by far is its softer (cabochon) variety. This yields gemstones that have bright red or orange spangles (minute crystals) suspended in a nearly colorless background in a parallel fashion, giving the whole a rich golden or redish brown color. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Tasty Cake
items.note: A well-sealed cake box with intact cake

items.name: Tattoo Kit
items.note: Ritualistic tattooing kit

items.name: Tchazar Gem
items.note: Tchazar is the common name given to aragonite, a straw-yellow gemstone found in elongated, prism-shaped crystal form. It is soft and fragile, and requires skilled cutting to yield faceted gems.. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Teak Mask
items.note: A cracked mask of teak wood, colored in (chipped) blue and red paint depicting the face of a fairy (4sp)

items.name: Teakwood Quarterstaff
items.note: An ornately carved quarterstaff of teakwood. The end is carved into the likeness of a dragon’s head, with (painted red) flame issuing from its mouth and the butt end carved into an entwining serpent-like tail. (5gp) (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Terrible Thermometer
items.note:
This thermometer has a long, silver blade and a pommeled wooden handle. "Come here, monster! I just want to take your temperature!" | 1d6 Piercing
Finesse, Light

This shortsword can be used to tell the temperature of anything it is embedded in. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: The Book of Monster Recipes
items.note: This leather-bound recipe book appears to be aimed at adventurers who aren't satified with merely fighting monsters and taking their treasure. It features a mixed selection of recipies, with side-notes on how best to prepare the remains of each creature included. "Braized Dragon Liver and Onions", "Eye of the Beholder Soup", "Rust Monster Jerky" and "Gelatinous Cube Jelly" are particular standouts. | Allows the reader to prepare meals from most common monsters without poisoning themselves. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: The Headbanger
items.note: This impromptu club was once a lute, but has since been smashed and clumsily repaired many times. Perhaps the bard who originally owned this played a few too many of the wrong note. | 1d8 Bludgeoning - Simple - Two-Handed (Source: Fable)

items.name: The Neverburning Torch
items.note: The neverburning torch. A jet black torch, with an inscription in gold upon its side: “Only in your hour of darkest need will I light.” All attempts – magical or otherwise – to light the torch will fail, but if the character carrying the torch ever finds himself upon the brink of death, the torch will flare to life.

items.name: The Recipes and Ruminations of One Dradeel of Tethir
items.note: The Recipes and Ruminations of One Dradeel of Tethir is a book with food recipes from Dradeel. Even a casual look will inform the reader that he was a wizard with an addled mind. (Source: BGII)

items.name: The Three Rules to Poisoncraft
items.note: This tatty old tome is missing a number of pages, leaving whatever the third rule of Poisoncraft completely unknown. Hopefully you'll manage with the first two, unlike whomever owned this book before you. | Grants advantage to Alchemy checks. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Tigerseye Ring
items.note: You find a barrel filled with broken crockery. Near the bottom is a mouse nest in which, amidst the tangled mouse fur, lays a silver ring set with a polished tigerseye.

items.name: Tobacco Cask
items.note: A small cedar cask of dwarven pipe tobacco. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Tome of Clear Thought
items.note: A typical Tome of Clear Thought contains a collection of esoteric and scholarly literature, often involving obscure mathematical or algebraic formulae or the occasional reference to inter-planar biology.. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Tome of Understanding
items.note: A typical Tome of Understanding contains a sober, if dry analysis of legal battles, historic events, government policies, philosophical treatises and theories of magic and metaphysics. | Grants advantage to History checks. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Top Secret Battle Monkeys
items.note: Box of ‘Top Secret Battle Monkeys’

items.name: Tourmaline Chain
items.note: A delicate silver chain set with a tourmaline.

items.name: Treasure Map I
items.note: Piece of Legendary weapon & map with possible locations for other pieces

items.name: Treasure Map II
items.note: A peg leg inscribed with a pirate’s treasure map. The map is either false or its treasure has long since been plundered. If you wish to provide the PCs with a true map, then one may be found in the hollow compartment within the leg.

items.name: Trident
items.note:
1d6 piercing
thrown, versatile (1d8) (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Triple Harness
items.note: A leather harness bearing three short quivers (sized for quarrels, rather than arrows). The first holds a dozen red-fletched quarrels, the second a dozen green fletched quarrels, and the third has a dozen white fletched quarrels.

items.name: Troll Horn
items.note: When blown, any trolls within a mile or so will approach the players | When blown, any trolls within a mile or so will approach the players

items.name: Troll Oboe
items.note: An oboe-like musical instrument made from a troll’s femur.

items.name: Unfinished Zircon Gem
items.note: This gemstone has not yet been finished, but even in its unpolished state it's bright cyan gleam is arresting to the eye. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Unicorn Pipes
items.note: A set of panpipes carved from the bones of a unicorn. When played, they do not make the slightest sound.

items.name: Unspoiled Golden Apples
items.note: A bowl full of golden apples found in the midst of ruins which have long been left desolate and uninhabited. They are quite edible and unspoiled. If they are taken beyond the ruins, they will lose their golden sheen and appear – in all respects – as normal, red apples. So long as they remain within the ruins, however, they are golden, and will not age or rot.

items.name: Unusable Scroll
items.note: A tattered, and sadly unusable, scroll fragment, inscribed with a spell unknown to any living mage.

items.name: Usk Brandy
items.note: A five gallon cask of usk brandy.

items.name: Vial of Antitoxin
items.note: A creature that drinks this vial of liquid gains advantage on saving throws against poison for 1 hour. It confers no benefit to undead or constructs. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Vials of Oil
items.note: Seventy-three clay vials of oil (1 pint each).

items.name: Wand Chamber
items.note: Wand Chamber – fashioned to look like a scroll case, it contains 1d12 slots to hold wands – think of it as a circular bandolier for your everyday wizard

items.name: Wand of Pillows
items.note: At any time, if the users says "Floomp" a pillow will magically appear. After the eighth pillow apparates, they start simply teleporting to the user in order.

items.name: War Pick
items.note: 1d8 Piercing (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Warhammer
items.note:
1d8 bludgeoning
versatile (1d10) (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Warning Box
items.note: Box with runic markings for destroy/to be destroyed containing cursed object

items.name: Water Damaged Ledger
items.note: Water damaged ledger – hidden location

items.name: Waterstar Gem
items.note: Waterstar is also called achroite or colourless tourmaline and is rare in the Realms. This stone is riddled with flaws and inclusions and hence only a small portion is fit for cutting. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Weapon Cabinet
items.note: A weapons cabinet, unlocked and immovable, containing: 12 score arrows (1gp/score), 40 javelins (1gp each), 120 crossbow bolts (1sp/each), and a short bow (30gp).

items.name: Whalebone Scrimshaw
items.note: An elaborate, whalebone scrimshaw carving of intertwined orbs, each showing the legend of creation story of one of the seven civilized religions, roughly 10” in diameter.

items.name: Whip
items.note:
1d4 slashing
finesse, reach (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: White Ivory Cane
items.note: A cane of the purest white ivory, decorated with ruins unknown to modern scholars, and topped with a handle of polished amber. Trapped within the amber is a strange creature unlike any the PCs have ever seen before.

items.name: White Marble Ball
items.note: A small ball of white marble – roughly three inches across – which hovers in the air until it is touched or disturbed, at which point it falls to the floor and ceases to exhibit any supernatural properties.

items.name: Wooden Wind Chimes
items.note: A set of wooden wind chimes which plays a different tune depending upon the direction of the wind which disturbs it.

items.name: Wrought Iron Sign
items.note: A large, wrought-iron sign crafted to read: “Beware of Wyvern” in a bold script.

items.name: Ziose Gemstone
items.note: Ziose is the name given by sages to a particular facet-grade variety of ziosite; a rare mineral that yields cut stones that flash three vivid hues depending on how the light catches them; purple, blue, and red, or purple, green, and red. Very large (fist- or foot-sized) gems are found and are prized for use in pendants and brooches. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Zircon Gemstone
items.note: Zircon is a brownish crystal found in igneous (volcanic) rocks. Zircon attains its pale blue shade with heating and cutting (usually facet-cut). (Source: SRD)

items.name: Zombie Dust
items.note: A small, brown glass jar, sealed with wax, with a gray dust inside. A white label is affixed to the jar, and reads: “Zombie Dust” in a shaky script. (It is zombie dust.)

items.name: 1d20 Ammunition +1
items.note: You have a bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this piece of magic ammunition. The bonus is determined by the rarity of the ammunition. Once it hit a target, the ammunition is no longer magical. Value listed is for individual units. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Adamantine Armor
items.note: This suit of armor is reinforced with adamantine, one of the hardest substances in existence. While you're wearing it, any critical hit against you becomes a normal hit. | Medium, Heavy (Not Hide) (Source: DMG)

items.name: Adventurer's Robe
items.note:
This robe has been specially enchanted to meet the eclectic needs of the adventuring mage. Aside from protecting the wearer from various forms of crushing damage, it also provides protection from the basilisk's petrying gaze and polymorphing powers of rival mages. As with other such robes, the Adventurer's Robe can only be worn by those engaged in the wizardly profession. | AC: 11 + Dex Modifier
Disadvantage on Stealth
+ 1 bonus vs. petrification & polymorph (Source: Baldur's Gate II)

items.name: Aeger's Hide
items.note:
This heavy and thick bear hide is all that remains of the fabled Aeger. Several hundred years ago, the Aeger, a giant bear, terrorized hundreds of small villages up and down the Sword Coast. The bear was invulnerable to fire, cold, or acid. Finally the young men and women of several villages banded together, forming a militia almost two hundred people strong. The Aeger was surrounded and finally slain. Less than a dozen villagers survived, and they carved the Aeger's hide amongst themselves. Out of one of the pieces this armor was created. | Medium Armor
Armor Class: 12 + Dex Modifier (max 2)
Provides +15% resistance to fire, cold, and acid
Render's it's wearer immune to confusion (Source: Baldur's Gate II)

items.name: Air Elemental Statue
items.note: This enchanted statuette depicts the swirling form of an air elemental. When stood close, you can feel a cooling breeze swirling around the statuette. (Source: Baldur's Gate II)

items.name: Alchemy Jug
items.note: This ceramic jug appears to be able to hold a gallon of liquid and weighs 12 pounds whether full or empty. Sloshing sounds can be heard from within the jug when it is shaken, even if the jug is empty. You can use an action and name one liquid from the table below to cause the jug to produce the chosen liquid. Afterward, you can uncork the jug as an action and pour that liquid out, up to 2 gallons per minute. The maximum amount of liquid the jug can produce depends on the liquid you named. Once the jug starts producing a liquid, it can't produce a different one, or more of one that has reached its maximum, until the next dawn. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Allmagic Scrolls
items.note: These pieces of unweatherd, thick parchment are lined by a thin string of gold woven into the paper. To anyone who cannot read magic, they are always blank and featureless. However, someone who can read magic realizes that these scrolls can contain spell charges—of ANY type of spell, rather than just Arcane. Paladins, Clerics, and Druids, rejoice! Two charges of the spell must be spent to write one to the scroll, and some scrolls might be found with magic already written to them. These scrolls, once written to, can be cast free of charge—but the scroll is consumed in the process. Store those odd utility spells on Allmagic Scrolls, and never waste an unused daily charge again! (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Amber Gemstone
items.note: An amber gemstone of watery gold colouring. It has been polished so that you can see a small petrified leaf suspended inside. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Amethyst Drinking Cup
items.note: An ornate cup fashioned out of a deep purple Amethyst. This material was probably chosen as many believe it to prevent intoxication. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Amulet of Proof Against Detection and Location
items.note: While wearing this amulet, you are hidden from divination magic. You can't be targeted by such magic or perceived through magical scrying sensors. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Ancient Coins
items.note: A cache of ancient coins left from the elder days of the world and made by a civilization utterly alien to the values of today: They are carved from bloodstone, moonstone, and jacinth – with unknown faces and unreadable runes decorating their surface. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Ancient Gold Cube
items.note: Ancient gold cube – quite encumbering. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Ancient Mummy
items.note: An ancient mummy which was given full burial rites and laid within a stately sarcophagus. Arranged on five pedestals around the sarcophagus are the canopic jars in which the mummy’s vital organs were placed. Although the mummy is not of the undead, opening these jars will reveal that its organs continue to function: The heart beats, the lungs fill with air and empty again, and so forth. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Ancient Wand Box
items.note: A collection case holding fifty spent wands – crafted by the finest craftsman and enchanted by the mightiest wizards of a bygone age, but now reduced to mere wood, bone, and glass. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Animal Figurines
items.note: A small box of turquoise animal figurines. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Anthracite Chair
items.note: In this room of the dungeon, there is an elaborate arm chair carved entirely of a single piece of coal. It has been inlaid with silver runes that age has rendered unreadable. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Armageddon Diorama
items.note: A tiny diorama made of oak and silver, depicting a prophecy of the last days of the world in vivid detail. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Armentarious' Scroll Tube
items.note: A very large scroll tube containing the plans for many of the ancient war engines of Armentarious. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Astrologer's Chart
items.note: An astrologer’s chart, inscribed with the finest instruments and the greatest precision upon a vellum which is both supple and expensive. Close inspection by those with the proper knowledge, however, reveals that these charts were constructed around an entirely different night sky – and, possibly, a calendrical system and length of year unlike anything known upon this world. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Aumurille's Chest
items.note: A miniature golden chest with tiny emeralds lining the top that trace out a mage’s sigil. The chest is crafted in exquisite detail. (This is the material component of a “Secret Chest” (from the spell of the same name) for the ruthless lady-mage Aumurille, who will likely want it back....) (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Axe of Blood
items.note: crimson red axe that deals 1d6 slashing damage and 1d6 bludgeoning damage. Each hit restores +1 hp to wielding regardless of damage dealt

items.name: Bag of Holding
items.note: This bag has an interior space considerably larger than its outside dimensions, roughly 2 feet in diameter at the mouth and 4 feet deep. The bag can hold up to 500 pounds, not exceeding a volume of 64 cubic feet. The bag weighs 15 pounds, regardless of its contents. Retrieving an item from the bag requires an action. If the bag is overloaded, pierced, or torn, it ruptures and is destroyed, and its contents are scattered in the Astral Plane. If the bag is turned inside out, its contents spill forth, unharmed, but the bag must be put right before it can be used again. Breathing creatures inside the bag can survive up to a number of minutes equal to 10 divided by the number of creatures (minimum 1 minute), after which time they begin to suffocate. Placing a bag of holding inside an extradimensional space created by a Heward's handy haversack, portable hole, or similar item instantly destroys both items and opens a gate to the Astral Plane. The gate originates where the one item was placed inside the other. Any creature within 10 feet of the gate is sucked through it to a random location on the Astral Plane. The gate then closes. The gate is one-way only and can't be reopened. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Bag of Saffron
items.note: A small bag of saffron. It can be used to flavor the parties travel rations, but is probably more valuable as a trade good. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Bag of Tricks, Grey
items.note: This ordinary gray cloth bag appears to be empty. Reaching inside the bag, however, reveals the presence of a small, fuzzy object. You can use an action to pull the fuzzy object from the bag and throw it up to 20 feet. When the object lands, it transforms into a creature you determine by rolling a d8 and consulting the table that corresponds to the bag’s color. The creature vanishes at the next dawn or when it is reduced to 0 hit points. The creature is friendly to you and your companions, and it acts on your turn. You can use a bonus action to command how the creature moves and what action it takes on its next turn, or to give it general orders, such as to attack your enemies. In the absence of such orders, the creature acts in a fashion appropriate to its nature. Once three fuzzy objects have been pulled from the bag, the bag cannot be used again until the next dawn. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Bagpipe of Invisibility
items.note: A wind instrument consisting of a reed melody pipe and from one to five drones with air supplied continuously either by a bag with valve-stopped mouth tube or by bellows. The bag itself is patterned in red and blue. | Renders the user invisible whilst the bagpipes are being played. (Source: /u/dustybullets)

items.name: Bat Sack
items.note: Witch’s Mad Sack of Bats – releases swarm of bats (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Battle Banner
items.note: A famous battle banner, long lost, smells badly (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Bear Claw Boots
items.note: A pair of dark leather boots upon which a curious gnomish mechanism has been fastened around. There is a small lever on the side of the ankle that, when toggled, extends on each boot a set of sharp mountaineering claws. The boots are sized for medium humanoid but the mechanism itself would be easy enough for a blacksmith to scale up or down. | The mountaineering claws provide a +2 circumstance bonus to climb checks. This bonus can be used in addition to a Climber's Kit. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Behir Horn
items.note: A stoppered behir horn containing 300 gp worth of powdered electrum (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Belt of the Shippey
items.note: A dark, water-worn leather belt embossed with an intricate seascape all around its girth. It has a polished golden buckle, as well as the name “Jadmek” carved into the front. It confers a +2 bonus to all Use Rope checks, as well as a +2 bonus to knowledge of anything maritime. Any encounter with pirates or seafaring merchants might prove interesting if the NPCs spot this belt. (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Bird Cage
items.note: An antique golden bird cage with an electrum filigree. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Black Lotus Pollen
items.note: Four half-pound bricks of compressed Black Lotus pollen | Potent poison. Very valuable to...the right people. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Black Mountain Stout Recipe
items.note: A 1’ x 3’ bronze plate bearing the ancient, and secret recipe for Black Mountain Stout (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Blank Contract
items.note: A contract with blank parts. Can be modified into any sort of deal you wish. The first party to break the contract is blasted with a beam from above (d12, ignore armour) and the contract dissolves into shredded parchment.

items.name: Blood Amulet
items.note: A bronze circle held by a red ribbon. | This amulet is constantly covered in blood. Any attempt to wash the blood off is unsuccessful, and the amulet must be wrapped in a waterproof cloth to prevent anything it is kept in frow slowly becoming drenched in blood.

items.name: Blood Waterskin
items.note: Waterskin of lamb’s blood

items.name: Blue Dragon Figurine
items.note: A finely crafted figurine of a blue dragon, rampant, done in wood and painted.

items.name: Boomerang of Retrieval
items.note:
This otherwise normal looking boomerang has a sense of arcana deep within it's dark oak core. Researching it's history will reveal that this is, in fact, one of many created by a local wizard who was frustrated at being unable to throw a normal boomerang in such a way that it returned naturally. | 1d6 Bludgeoning (+1 force damage) - Thrown (Range: 30/90)
Special: Does not require direct line of sight, due to a curved trajectory. 
Unlike a normal boomerang, the boomerang of retrieval will always return to you after you make a ranged attack with it, even if you hit. (Source: D&D Wiki)

items.name: Boots of Elvenkind
items.note: While you wear these boots, your steps make no sound, regardless of the surface you are moving across. You also have advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks that rely on moving silently. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Boots of Striding and Springing
items.note: A pair of tan boots which are fastened around the foot with three adjustable belts. The inner sole of these boots feels springy to the touch and seems to mould itself around the user's foot for a fit so comfortable it's like walking on the clouds. | While you wear these boots, your walking speed becomes 30 feet, unless your walking speed is higher, and your speed isn't reduced if you are encumbered or wearing heavy armor. In addition, you can jump three times the normal distance, though you can't jump farther than your remaining movement would allow. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Boots of the Winterlands
items.note: A pair of hide boots lined with dense, warm white fur. The eyelet for each bootlace is patterned with small gemstones and the sole of the boot is spiked so as to benefit the user over icy and unstable terrain. These furred boots are snug and feel quite warm. | See DMG page 156. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Box o' Diseases
items.note: An ornate box containing corked vials of different diseases

items.name: Box of Evil Miniatures
items.note: A collection of humanoid figurines, each poised in an unspeakable act of evil. They are packed in box which has been wrapped in lamb’s wool.

items.name: Bracers of Archery
items.note: While wearing these bracers, you have proficiency with the longbow and shortbow, and you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls on ranged attacks made with such weapons. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Bracers of Binding
items.note: The bracers appear to be Bracers of Specialization, but when donned, bind the wearer wrists together, making it quite difficult to wield a weapon or cast a spell. These bracers are sometimes offered as rewards for services rendered by the Drow. The recipient, believing them to be very powerful, will often don them before examinating them. At this point, the drow find it amusing to conjure a hook horror. | lower damage by 5 points, increase spell failure to 75% and give the wearer a +5 penalty to THACO. Since they are cursed, they can't be unequiped without a Remove Curse spell or the services of a priest (Source: BGII)

items.name: Brass Dragon Egg
items.note: A delicate brass dragon egg; hollowed out and painted into a seascape, resting on a wooden stand.

items.name: Brass Mug
items.note: A plain brass mug with a surprisingly ornate jade inlay. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Bronze Caltrops
items.note: A cask full of bronze caltrops.

items.name: Bronze Crown
items.note: An old bronze crown, once inlaid with gemstones. They have since been picked out, but the crown itself is wonderfully cast. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Bronze Tower Shield
items.note:
A tower shield of bronze, cast into the likeness of a chimera’s heads and enameled to provide the appropriate colors. | Wielding this shield increases your Armor Class by 3. You can benefit from only one shield at a time.
Disadvantage on Stealth (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Brooch of Shielding
items.note: While wearing this brooch, you have resistance to force damage, and you have immunity to damage from the magic missile spell. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Broom of Flying
items.note: This wooden broom functions like a mundane broom until you stand astride it and speak its command word. It then hovers beneath you and can be ridden in the air. The broom stops hovering when you land. You can send the broom to travel alone to a destination within 1 mile of you if you speak the command word, name the location, and are familiar with that place. The broom comes back to you when you speak another command word, provided that the broom is still within 1 mile of you. | It has a flying speed of 50 feet. It can carry up to 400 pounds, but its flying speed becomes 30 feet while carrying over 200 pounds. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Bull's Head Ring
items.note: A silver ring shaped into the likeness of a bull’s head.

items.name: Bullette Horn Barding
items.note: A complete barding for a war horse made of Bullette horn plates.

items.name: Cap of Water Breathing
items.note: The word “STYX” is sewn into the inner headband of this cap. | While wearing this cap underwater you can speak its command word as an action to create a bubble of air arround your head. It allows you to breathing normally underwater. This bubble stays with you until you speak the command word again, the cap is removed or you are no longer underwater. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Ceramic Jars
items.note: 4 ceramic canopic jars, holding the stomach, intestines, lungs, and liver of a long-ago monarch. Each has a golden lid with a likeness of a paunchy, hook-nosed man with pale skin and a squint.

items.name: Charm of Frost
items.note: grants water-walking abilities, but requires concentration. Anything that disrupts a concentration spell disrupts the charm. 10 ft radius of ice

items.name: Chess Set
items.note: A large, boxed chess set. All the pieces are small, dead pixies somehow sealed within glass.

items.name: Chrysoberyl Gem
items.note: Chrysoberyl is a hard, transparent, green gem which is usually facet-cut. It is said that Chrysoberyl has ties to the outer planes and hence its use with battling demonic possession and the undead. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Clàrsach Harp
items.note: A clàrsach harp in the style of the northern barbarians, but crafted with an elegance which suggests the decadence of a great civilization. Its strings are almost too fine to be perceived with the naked eye, and when they are played they resonate with the force of a full orchestra. | Add your proficiency bonus to all Performance (Charisma) checks made when playing the harp.

items.name: Cloak of Partial Shadow
items.note: A normal looking black cloak with a high collar. When activated it gives off small puffs of black smoke that help obscure the wearer. | Speak the command word to activate the cloak and receive advantage on your next stealth roll. The wearer can only benefit from the effect once. It lasts for up to 6 seconds. (Source: AdamLapka)

items.name: Companion Cube
items.note: A Companion Cube: It was created by a wizard who had stuck himself in a tower to show complete dedication to his studies. However, after several decades he became so lonely that he chose to make a friend... literally. Using what little non-component materials he had laying around his tower, he created a small cube and gave it sentience. It was able to communicate telepathically with anyone who made a link with the cube. Unfortunately, the wizard eventually died and the cube became lonely. Unable to travel and find a new friend, the cube simply sat there and waited for anyone to come along. Anyone seeing the cube would be compelled to be with the cube and have it in their possession. Upon holding the cube, they would feel the need to keep it with them at all times, and protect it as if it were their own child. Little by little, the connection between the cube and it's possessor would grow, until the two would be inseparable. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Coral Circlet
items.note: A dark crimson Coral gemstone fitted into a golden circlet. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Crayon Torches
items.note: This set of 12 torches burn different colors. There are two sets of Red, Yellow, Blue, Green, Orange and Purple torches.

items.name: Crystal Sword
items.note: When used this sword will deliver 1 instant hit for max damage, including bonuses. Once used, the sword shatters into crystal shards. Anyone within one square must roll reflex or take 1d4 damage from shard pieces. | 1d8 Slashing - Versatile (1d10)

items.name: Dagger of Slicing
items.note:
Dagger of slicing – ignores armor bonus | 1d4 Piercing
Finesse - Light - Thrown: 20/60

items.name: Death Talker Charm
items.note: Charm that tells 1 truth about a creature killed in the area

items.name: Debt of a Soul
items.note: Debt of a Soul – contract of soul ownership (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Deck of Cards
items.note: An ancient deck of cards with five suits – clovers, swords, clubs, hearts, and dragons.

items.name: Decrepit Skeleton in Armor
items.note: The skeletal remains of a long-dead warrior, wearing a suit of plate armor which has long since tarnished. The name of every warrior to ever die by the last wearer’s hand has been inscribed upon the front and back of the chest plate, covering nearly every inch of its surface with chaotically written, black runes.

items.name: Devil Salts
items.note: Awakens any creature from sleep or unconsciousness, but they flail and scream in terror for a few seconds first. They have no memory of this afterwards.

items.name: Dragon Balm
items.note: Dragon balm – reduced heat damage (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Dragon Statuette
items.note: A statuette of a dragon, forged from the finest mithril, which responds to yes or no questions which are posed to it in draconic. The answers given may appear prophetic or oracular, but are actually random.

items.name: Druid's Magnet
items.note: A wood chip which acts as a magnet, despite its non-metallic nature.

items.name: Drums of Endurance
items.note: Once per day, give +10ft to movement, and +1 to attack and damage rolls to all allies.

items.name: Dryad Bomb
items.note: A so-called "Dryad Bomb", called such because when thrown it creates a mess of magically grown tangles and vines. Appears to be a glowing orange orb surrounded by a series of vines and roots. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Duergar Compass
items.note: This compass is carved out of heavy, hard Petrebony (a wood that grows in caves). A brass needle sits in the center. However, this compass' needle points East, not North. There are no markings on its surface. (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Dust of Appearance
items.note: Stack of 4, user covers 20ft radius in magic dust that shows anything that is hidden or dispellable.

items.name: Dust of Coughing and Sneezing
items.note: Magical dust that makes anyone cough and sneeze (combat)

items.name: Dust of Dispelling
items.note: stack of 4, user covers 20ft radius in magic dust that shows anything that is hidden or dispellable.

items.name: Dwarven Bomb
items.note: Source: Uberbeard

items.name: Edible Greatsword
items.note: Works like a normal greatsword. Great tasting and low in calories! Regenerates 6 inches per day. | 2d6 Slashing - Heavy - Two-Handed (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Eldritch Flint & Steel
items.note: Eldritch flint & steel – 10’ reach – blinding/minor burns

items.name: Elven Bomb
items.note: An orange-yellow shell with small claw-like protrusions. This appears more to have been grown rather than constructed. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Elven Crescent Blade
items.note:
A striking weapon defined by its deeply curved, moon-shaped blade and favoured by Drow. | 2d6 Slashing
Heavy - Two-handed

If you have the Exotic Weapon Master feat, the elven crescent blade gains the finesse property. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Elven Engraved Combs
items.note: An elegant carved comb of oak, engraved with the name Aethelren and decorated with twin dragons. Elsewhere, a similar comb of ash, engraved with the name Ilthorien and decorated with twin unicorns, can be found.

items.name: Empty Mason Jar
items.note: This magical mason jar is made of uneven, lumpy, blue-green glass with a copper lid. No matter what is placed in this transparent jar, it will always appear to be empty from the outside--so long as the lid is on. On the lid is pressed “calasse,” Elven for “clean.” It cannot hold more than its actual volume. (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Enchanted Apple
items.note: A purple-colored apple. | This enchanted apple has all the properties of a potion of Cure Light Wounds. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Exploding Gold Coins
items.note: Exploding gold coins (Looks legit. Should avoid contact with real gold coins for safety...)

items.name: Exploding Rations
items.note: Exploding rations – breathe weapon 1d6 hours for 1 use of color theme type

items.name: F**k You Fireworks
items.note: These appear to be normal Gnomish fireworks (although even those might seem unique and valuable to anyone unfamiliar with Gnomish ingenuity) but when they are eventually set off they spell out giant obscenities across the night's sky. It seems, in retrospect, that they may have been created by a rather angry Gnome. Sadly, whomever created the fireworks and who they were originally intended for will forever remain a mystery. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Finely Woven Rug
items.note: A finely made 10’ x 12’ wool floor rug. The center of the rug depicts a scene of siege warfare. A Halfling is being flung over a city wall by a trebuchet. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Flask of Nightmare Ale
items.note: Half-full flask of nightmare ale – 1 sip = 1d4 hours fear check per hour; full measure = 1d4 hours horrifying hallucinations vs DC save for early retirement

items.name: Flask of St. Cuthbert
items.note: A pair of steel flasks, sealed with wax bearing the symbol of St. Cuthbert. (Holy water, 25 gp each)

items.name: Fundial
items.note: A sundial which runs backwards.

items.name: Garnet Gemstone
items.note: Garnet is a general class of crystals ranging from deep red to violet in colour. a typical cut for this rare isometric gem is 12 to 24 sides, with the very rare 36 sides being known from time to time. it is rumored a 48-sided garnet exists although none have ever been able to track down its location. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Giant Boomerang
items.note:
This massive boomerang requires two hands by medium sized humanoids. This may have been specially designed for an ogre, or giant. It has sharp metal blades that run down the side of each wing. The blades on the inner curves make it a bit tricky to wield. | 1d8 Bludgeoning - Thrown (Range: 30/90) - Two-Handed
Special: Does not require direct line of sight, due to a curved trajectory. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Giant-King's Necklace
items.note: Giant-King’s platinum necklace with crimson diamond worn by dragon

items.name: Giant's Arrow
items.note: A huge arrow, with a steel tip and copper wire inlaid spirally down the shaft. To a medium or large creature, it behaves just like a javelin. However, when thrown, it whistles loudly. | 1d6 Piercing - Simple - Thrown - Range: 30/120 (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Glaive
items.note:
A three bladed disk which can either be used in melee or as a thrown weapon. The circular handle is made from dark wood and has the design of an owl's head in the middle. | 1d4 Slashing - Finesse - Light - Thrown (Range: 30/60)
Special: The weapon returns to the thrower's position after an attack and can be caught with a free action. (Source: D&D Wiki)

items.name: Glaive
items.note: This is a short pole-arm resembling a Fauchard or Guan do, with an additional spike for thrusting. It deals slashing damage when wielded in one hand and piercing damage when wielded in two hands. | 1d6 Slashing - Versatile (1d8 Piercing) (Source: D&D Wiki)

items.name: Globe of Invulnerability
items.note: Globe of Invulnerability (It's a snowglobe. Not even the gods themselves can destroy it.)

items.name: Gloves of Slapping
items.note: Gloves that improve a skill but will randomly force the user to slap an ally/NPC during conversations at the DM's discretion. (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Glowing Longsword
items.note:
A longsword made from a single piece of a dull, almost glossy, metal. It feels slightly heavier than expected and the handle is always warm to the touch. | 1d8 Slashing - Versatile (1d10)

Whenever the sword is wielded by whoever attuned to it the whole sword begins to emanate a soft glow. This creates dim light in a 5ft radius around it. This effect stops if the sword is dropped or otherwise stops being in direct contact with the wielder. (Source: Lamp_05)

items.name: Glowing Shortsword
items.note:
A shortsword made from a single piece of a dull, almost glossy, metal. It feels slightly heavier than expected and the handle is always warm to the touch. | 1d6 Piercing
Finesse - Light

Whenever the sword is wielded by whoever attuned to it the whole sword begins to emanate a soft glow. This creates dim light in a 5ft radius around it. This effect stops if the sword is dropped or otherwise stops being in direct contact with the wielder. (Source: Lamp_05)

items.name: Gnome Bomb
items.note: An ornate, egg-shaped device made with cast bronze. There is a pin in the top of the device with an etched note indicating the following: PULL THYS PINNE AND WAITE TENNE COUNTS BEFORRE BOOME. When the pin is pulled, an urgent ticking sound begins to eminate from the device. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Gnome Camo Paint
items.note: This paint provides no protection, but grants 10 to disguise. The only way it works, however, is if the wearer is wearing no clothes, and covered head to toe in the stuff. It's hilarious when a disguise check fails, and they realize they are staring at a naked Gnome.

items.name: Gnome Timepiece
items.note: A magical time-keeping device produced by a Gnomish artisan. Opening this circular locket allows you to check the current time based on where a delicate hand is pointing. On one half of the circular face is an ornate night's sky with a crescent moon indicating midnight. On the other half is a blue sky, with a bright sun indicating mid-day. It's unclear how the magic has been infused with the device, as no arcane presence is detectable, but pulling the silver panel off the back of the locket simply exposes a number of delicate, ticking gears. Gnome magic is compicated. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: God Ring
items.note: Ring of a god

items.name: Gold Bracelet
items.note: A golden bracelet. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Gold Ring
items.note: A small gold ring set with bloodstones. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Golden Arrows
items.note: A quiver of golden arrows, They are devoid of supernatural properties, but despite their unusual composition will perform as normal arrows would.

items.name: Golden Tablet
items.note: A tablet of pure gold, inscribed with the core rites and beliefs of a venerable religion. Careful study of this tablet, however, will reveal subtle – but important – differences between these ancient practices and the current practices of the religion in question.

items.name: Gravedigger
items.note:
1d4 Bludgeoning
Attack with advantage and 1d10 against undead enemies

items.name: Great Axe
items.note:
A hardwood handle topped with a heavy doublesided axe blade. It's construction is immaculate, and it's design is so archetypal you can't help but think, "Wow, what a great axe." | 1d8 Slashing - (Versatile 1d10)
+1 Attack Rolls (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Greataxe of Blindness
items.note:
This is a large greataxe, with a steel shaft and silver floating blade much like the Telescoping Glaive. | 1d14 Slashing - Martial - Heavy, Two-Handed
When raising this axe to swing the wielder suffers 1 minute of total blindness. (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Greedy Bag of Holding
items.note: This appears to be a common cloth sack about 2 feet by 4 feet in size. | This functions as a normal Bag of Holding, but for every item you wish to remove from the bag you must put in a silver piece. Any money placed into the bag dissapears almost immediately. It's unclear where the coins go when placed into the bag. (Source: William Orfanakos)

items.name: Griffon Bomb
items.note: A metallic egg-shaped weapon which, when thrown, sprouts magical wings and hovers above it's intended target. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Heart Shaped Token
items.note: A small, heart-shaped token carved out of rose-colored sandstone, holding the mad soul of a princess who was trapped there by a sorcerer many centuries ago. Her body remains in the hidden royal sepulchers of her homeland, perfectly preserved by ancient magic.

items.name: Helm of Comprehending Languages
items.note: A fully enclosed helmet of matte grey metal with a small chainmail nape covering. Rather than a simple gap, two green gems have been embedded to give an eye-like appearance, and as additional flourish a set of elven ears have been moulded into each side. | While wearing this helm, you can use an action to cast the comprehend languages spell from it at will. (Source: SRD)

items.name: Hero Dolls
items.note: These start getting made after the party does something particularly heroic. Little cloth dolls that look like party members, when squeezed, the caster spouts illusionary flames, the cleric spouts prayers "Praise Avandra!", the fighter has kung-fu action where he swings his sword.

items.name: Hidden Blade Bracer
items.note:
Concealed punching dagger-bracer | 1d4 Piercing - Finesse, Light - Range: 20/60 
When you attack and hit a creature that is surprised with this dagger, it must make a Constitution saving throw DC 18. On a failed save, the target takes an extra 2d4 damage from your attack.

items.name: Holy Ice Symbol
items.note: An ornate block of ice carved into an ancient holy symbol. The carving has a subtle taint of magic to it, but it's not exactly clear what prevents it from melting. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Horn Coral Gem
items.note: Horn Coral, which is also called night coral, is a deep black coral, similar to angel's skin save for its solid color. It is incredibly difficult to work with, and is used in jewelry as a polished twig or branch of material, or is cut cabochon. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Hypnotic Spinning Top
items.note: a spinning top that causes all creatures that can see it, including the person spinning it, to become mesmerized by it and will do nothing but stand in place and stare at it until it stops spinning and falls. This effect is technically psionic

items.name: Illusionary Coin Bag
items.note: a bag that makes all coins in it appear as platinum. Coins revert to original form 5 minutes after being taken out of bag. Can not hold more than 50 coins. This means you do not actually gain any profit from using the bag, but you can trick people

items.name: Immovable Rod
items.note: This flat iron rod has a button on one end. You can use an action to press the button, which causes the rod to become magically fixed in place. Until you or another creature uses an action to push the button again, the rod doesn't move, even if it is defying gravity. The rod can hold up to 8,000 pounds of weight. More weight causes the rod to deactivate and fall. A creature can use an action to make a DC 30 Strength check, moving the fixed rod up to 10 feet on a success.

items.name: Ivory Statuette
items.note: A carved sculpture of jungle animals on an ivory tusk. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Jade Buttons
items.note: A small collection of antique Jade buttons. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Jade Statue
items.note: A Weather-worn jade statue depicting a desert beast known only to the people of the far south.

items.name: Jade Telescope
items.note: A telescope of jade and banded with bloodstone. A character looking through the telescope sees not the world around them, but a strange, alien panorama which – nonetheless – follows the motions of the telescope.

items.name: Jet Brooch
items.note: A deep black brooch set with a Jet gemstone. Rumor suggests a brooch of this nature helps prevent the evil eye. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Jewel Encrusted Sarcophagus
items.note: Jewel encrusted sarcophagus – mummy inside

items.name: Kazoo of Intimidation
items.note: When this Kazoo is played, it grants +2 to Intimidation checks. A +3 if the player has a kazoo in the room.

items.name: Large Snow Globe
items.note: A large snow globe, with a study base of polished oak, depicting a small farmhouse in the midst of a forest clearing. Shaking the globe causes the snow to gently fall, and as it does miniature figures exit the house and begin performing chores about the farmyard. The figures return to the house as the last flake falls.

items.name: Lava Rocks
items.note: Rocks stuffed with lava

items.name: Lance
items.note:
1d12 Piercing
Reach

You have disadvantage when you use a lance to attack a target within 5 feet of you. Also, a lance requires two hands to wield when you aren’t mounted. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Learning Languages by Chompsky
items.note: A book detailing the general process of learning a language, from both a practical and theoretical angle. Written by a talented linguist called Chompsky the Gnome. | Allows any natural speaker of a language to be considered an "instructor" of that language. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Leomund's Loot Rack
items.note: This is a rack of weapons, armor, books, wands, whatever you desire. It holds anywhere from 3-6 items, each usually magical in nature. Each can be examined visually and by a Detect Magic spell. However, as soon as one is touched, the others disappear—never to be found again. Multiple items cannot be removed at once. Optionally a password could dispel the magic, allowing all items to be removed. (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Longsword of Balanced Sums
items.note: The Longsword of Balanced Sums. Functions exactly like a generic +2 Longsword... except when you crit miss. Then it heals the target for half its max hit points. | 1d8+2 Slashing - Versatile (1d10+2)

items.name: Lucky Coin
items.note: You must have this item with you for 24 hours for it to become attuned to you. Once attuned, you may choose the result any time it is flipped, regardless of who actually flips it. Possible commands include heads, tails, and edge.

items.name: Lucky Dodge Belt
items.note: Once an encounter, the player can choose to roll a d20 and use that as their base AC instead of 10. Player must take the value of the roll.

items.name: Mace of Chance
items.note:
A simple wooden handle with a large stone macehead that has been shaped into a icosahedron. It is a somewhat unweildy weapon due to it's immense weight, but you would not wish to be the recipient of a solid blow. On each of it's twenty sides you can make out faded engravings, anyone who can read Celestial will identify these as numbers. | 1d20 Bludgeoning Damage
-5 to Attack Rolls (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Magic Litmus Paper
items.note: Litmus magic paper: This paper can be used as a standard action by rubbing it on an object. It either detects no magic and can be used again, or changed color and brightness to reflect the school(s) and strengths of magic on the object.

items.name: Meal Table
items.note: A stone table in the midst of the wilderness which is, nevertheless, always filled with a fresh meal whenever travelers come across it.

items.name: Medallion of Courage
items.note: Once per day, swift. remove AC of target granted by armor, last one turn. user takes penalty to AC equal to target for same duration.

items.name: Menesta's Book of Stored Skill
items.note: This magical book has no discernable writings on the outside, or on any page. As soon as it is opened up, ask the player for the first three skills that come to mind. As soon as they tell you, the first page of the book becomes a table of contents with three chapters. The names of the chapters are the three skills they mentioned. Turning to the page of a skill chapter causes the book to disappear in a colored burst of light—and leaves the user with a permanent +1 rank to said skill. If their ranks are maxed out, the book is wasted. (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Merchant's Dagger Box
items.note: A merchant’s leather case containing a selection of twenty well-made daggers of varying designs. | 1d4 Piercing - Finesse, Light - Range: 20/60

items.name: Minting Tools
items.note: A set of dies and hammers for minting the king's coinage.

items.name: Mithral Breastplate
items.note:
This armor consists of a fitted mithral chest piece worn with supple leather. Although it leaves the legs and arms relatively unprotected, this armor provides good protection for the wearer's vital organs while leaving the wearer relatively unencumbered. | Medium Armor
Armor Class: 14 + Dex Modifier (max 2) (Source: PHB)

items.name: Mithral Chain Mail Armor
items.note:
Made of interlocking metal rings, chain mail includes a layer of quilted fabric worn underneath the mail to prevent chafing and to cushion the impact of blows. The suit includes gauntlets. | Heavy Armor
Armour Class: 16 (Source: PHB)

items.name: Mithral Chain Shirt
items.note:
Made of interlocking metal rings, a chain shirt is worn between layers of clothing or leather. This armor offers modest protection to the wearer's upper body and allows the sound of the rings rubbing against one another to be muffled by outer layers. | Medium Armor
Armor Class: 13 + Dex Modifier (max 2) (Source: PHB)

items.name: Mithral Half Plate Armor
items.note:
Shaped mithral plates that cover most of the wearer's body. It does not include leg protection beyond simple greaves that are attached with leather straps. | Medium Armor
Armor Class: 15 + Dex Modifier (max 2) (Source: PHB)

items.name: Mithral Plate Armor
items.note:
Plate consists of shaped, interlocking metal plates to cover the entire body. A suit of plate includes gauntlets, heavy leather boots, a visored helmet, and thick layers of padding underneath the armor. Buckles and straps distribute the weight over the body. | Heavy Armor
Armour Class: 18 (Source: PHB)

items.name: Mithral Ring Mail Armor
items.note:
This armor is leather armor with heavy rings sewn into it. The rings help reinforce the armor against blows from swords and axes. Ring mail is inferior to chain mail, and it's usually worn only by those who can't afford better armor. | Heavy Armor
Armor Class: 14 (Source: PHB)

items.name: Mithral Scale Mail Armor
items.note:
This armor consists of a coat and leggings of leather covered with overlapping pieces of mithral, much like the scales of a fish. The suit includes gauntlets. | Medium Armor
Armor Class: 14 + Dex Modifier (max 2) (Source: PHB)

items.name: Mithral Splint Armor
items.note:
This armor is made of narrow vertical strips of metal riveted to a backing of leather that is worn over cloth padding. Flexible chain mail protects the joints. | Heavy Armor
Armour Class: 17 (Source: PHB)

items.name: Moonbar Gemstone
items.note: Moonbar is a pearly white, opaque gemstone, usually pale blue with green and gold mottling. Related in type to fire and black opals, but is only slightly more common. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Morph Blade
items.note: This blade can switching between piecing, slashing and bludgeoning damage as a bonus action.

items.name: Moss Earplugs
items.note: Two peanut-sized balls of moss sit atop gnarled wooden knobs. Placing one of them in each ear causes total deafness—or total hearing protection, whichever your preference. The player cannot hear anything, and must succeed on a DC 16 Will save to speak a single sentence comprehensibly. However, they are totally immune to all sonic attacks and songs and can fall asleep extremely well. The earplugs can be removed at will. (Source: /u/LittleDizzleDaGusha)

items.name: Neverfull Waterskin
items.note: Neverfull Waterskin (It's always half empty.)

items.name: Obsidian Dolphin
items.note: An obsidian figurine of a dolphin which is always wet to the touch.

items.name: Old Guard's Note
items.note: Within a rotten pouch found upon the body of a long dead soldier is a parchment with an unbroken seal. If the seal is broken and the message read, it warns its would-be recipient to guard against an eminent betrayal.

items.name: Orkon's Club
items.note:
An inscription in the smooth handle of this simple spiked mace indicates that it was once wielded by a diminuative Gnome warrior named Orkon. | 1d8 Bludgeoning
Light - Slow (Source: Fable)

items.name: Ornate Abacus
items.note: An abacus with counting beads made of ivory, silver, gold, and vitrified eye balls. (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Ornate Rapier
items.note: A rapier in an ornate, electrum-trimmed steel scabbard worked in swirl-patterns. | 1d8 Piercing - Martial - Finesse

items.name: Pearl
items.note: Pearls consist of layers of aragonite agonizingly formed around a bit of grit or other irritant inside oysters and other mollusks. The resulting pearl has a rich, deep luster. Most of the pearls in the realms are white, however there are varieties that are much more rare such as rainbow and black. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Porcelain Discs
items.note: One hundred and sixteen 3” white porcelain discs, each has a 1 ounce knob of electrum embedded in its center

items.name: Potion of False Bluff
items.note: A potion vial containing a sweet. honey-smelling golden syrup. There is a label on the vial written in common, which reads "Potion of Deception". | The drinker of the potion perceives that they are an amazing liar. They believe that every bluff, diplomacy, and intimidation check they make for the next 1d4 hours succeeds, whether it does or not. (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Potion of Harmless Flaying
items.note: A pearlescent green potion in a glass vial that seems to swirl playfully when started at. | 1d4 rounds after drinking this potion target produces the illusions of his/her innards becoming outards for 1d6 minutes. The illusion is entirely intangible, but nonetheless real looking. (Source: Sherwood Bosco)

items.name: Pouch of Healing Potions
items.note:
A large carry pouch made from the skin of a monkey. Inside are 1d6 Potions of Greater Healing. | You regain 4d4 + 4 hit points when you drink this potion. 
The potion's red liquid glimmers when agitated. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Pouch of Mushrooms
items.note: A small brown pouch that smells faintly musty. Inside it are 1d4 colourful mushrooms that are unrecognizable, even to a particularly knowledgeable alchemist or naturist. If determined, a painstaking delve through a herbalists library might identify the mushrooms as "sub-space mushrooms" in a madly written diary of a man claiming to be a deposed king called Cooper. | If eaten, each mushroom will increase the consumer's size to one greater than it currently is for an hour. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Puppet Theater
items.note: An elegant puppet theater. Although its wood is brightly polished and the red of its satin curtains is bright and fresh, the hand-painted sign upon its front (reading “The Wondrous Show of Punch and Judy” in carnival lettering) is chipped and faded. If the curtains are opened (using a set of finely woven pulley ropes which lie inside the theater), the puppets – stored in a concealed compartment “backstage” – will come to life and perform elaborate shows of their own volition.

items.name: Quicksilver Surprise
items.note: In a worn and decaying wall of mud bricks, a single black brick – polished and perfectly preserved – stands out in stark contrast. If the brick is pried out and broken open, it will reveal a quicksilver core.

items.name: Rabbit's Foot
items.note: A lucky rabbit’s foot which hangs from a golden chain. Although separated from the rest of the rabbit, the foot magically lives on: It will respond to touch, bleed if injured, and so forth.

items.name: Raven Feather Fans
items.note: A pair of pole mounted fans made of giant raven feathers

items.name: Raven Pen
items.note: A pen made from a raven’s feather. Through some magical means it writes continuously from an inexhaustible supply of golden ink upon whatever piece of parchment may be near by.

items.name: Ricochet Sling
items.note: Ricochet-sling – up to 4 targets (d4 for targets, attack roll each; first attack fail causes ricochet to end [unless a tie, in the case of a tie to AC the ricochet continues])

items.name: Ring of Bad Luck
items.note: This ring will appear to be a Ring of Good Luck without an appraise check of 25. It actually imbues the wearer -1 on all saves and is not removable for a full day without removing the enchantment.

items.name: Ring of Dubious Might
items.note: A silver ring, encrusted with a small topaz. Upon wearing it on their finger, the wearer feels incredibly strong and confident. However, entering combat, their confidence immediately dissipates, leaving them terrified. (Source: Jacos)

items.name: Ring of Fake Invisibility
items.note: A silver ring inlaid with a trasluscent pearl. There is an inscription in Elvish which reads, "To Elabella, perhaps now you will find your mirror less engrossing." | The wearer of this ring is unable to see themselves or anything they are carrying.

items.name: Ring of Good Luck
items.note: This ring imbues the wearer +1 on all saves.

items.name: Romeot and Julio
items.note: One of the most famous plays in the works of the famous Orcish bard "Villam Spearshaker". It tells the story of two starcrossed lovers whose familes are locked in a bitter rivalry. They attempt to run away together only to end up eaten by an ogre. The ogre then proceeds to eat most of the two feuding families, all of the side characters, and in a number of early renditions of the play a few members of the audience as well. A note to budding directors: Try not to cast an actual ogre as the ogre. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Romeot and Julio II
items.note: A sequel to the original play by the famous Orcish bard; Villam Speakshaker. Romeot and Julio (or at least, what's left of them) are ressurected as shambling zombies in service to a wealthy merchant. The play ends with the merchant attempting to extract a pound of flesh from Romeot to pay for a debt before getting eaten alive by the two zombies. Their final moments of the play are spent lovingly feeding each other his bloody entrails. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Salve of Silence
items.note: Salve of Silence – armor improving sound nullifier

items.name: Screaming Arrows
items.note: A quarry of 20 Screaming Arrows – intimidate/fear check

items.name: Sentient Dagger of Sneaking
items.note: This sentient dagger attempts to help anytime it's owner tries to sneak by whistling inconspicuously.  For some reason it seems to have been discarded. | 1d4 Piercing - Finesse, Light - Range: 20/60 (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Shell Paste Lotion
items.note: A small metal tin which has been carefully labelled. Instructions are included on the inside of the lid, "Smear on bare skin to grow a natural defence." The paste inside is white and gritty. It smells like coconut. | Smearing Shell Paste lotion on your bare skin will cause an exoskeleton to grow for 1d4 hours. This exoskeleton increases your AC by 1. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Shrunken Head
items.note: Shrunken head with command word – 1 ray spell per hour

items.name: Signet Ring of Gold
items.note: A large signet ring of gold, worked with a unicorn crest. Any commoner who dons the ring will suddenly discover its metal heated to unbearable levels – causing one hit point of damage per round. However, anyone with noble blood (however slight), or destined to become a noble at some point in the future, who does the same will suffer only a momentary flash of pain (with an accompanying 1 hp of damage). They will find that the unicorn crest has reshaped itself to their own heraldry, which has also been branded in miniature form upon the back of the finger. From this point forward, they can put on or remove the ring at will without any further ill effects.

items.name: Silk Robe
items.note: A long silk robe with golden embroidery. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Silver Necklace
items.note: This silver necklace has a gemstone pendant. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Skull Idol
items.note: Gold dipped idol of several skulls

items.name: Smoke of Deceit
items.note: One time use only, grants perfect hide and move silently rolls to all allies. effect is lost if moved within 10ft of enemy

items.name: Snakeskin Scimitar
items.note:
A scimitar with a gold and snakeskin hilt set with a citrine. | 1d6 Slashing
Finesse, Light (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: Solomon's Fishing Pole
items.note: A cursed fishing pole made of blackened ash. It will never catch a fish – although, if one attempts to use it unbaited, they will succeed in catching skeleton fish.

items.name: Sortida Compass
items.note: Brass dungeon compass, runic markings, always points to nearest exit.

items.name: Sphene Gem
items.note: Sphene is a soft, brittle gemstone (and, like scapra, easily worked by unskilled cutters) of various yellow to green shades, the most prized of these being a fine emerald green. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Spider-Silk Rope
items.note: Giant spider silk rope – sticks to most surfaces (including oiled gloves)

items.name: Spinel Necklace
items.note: A spun leather necklace of deep green Spinel beads. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Staff of Eulosus
items.note:
A slender staff of pale, living wood, that is unexpectedly light . Its surface is etched with spiralling runes that resemble rising currents of air, and when held, the engravings give off a faint, silvery glow—like moonlight caught in motion. | Once per day, standard. lifts target into air for 3 rounds. 
Enemy can make a reflex save each round to get out DC 10+User level. (Source: Aziraphale)

items.name: Staff of Force
items.note: once per day, standard. push target 10 ft the direction they are facing. once per day. If target is pushed into another creature, they deal 1d20 damage. to each other. If target is pushed into object, they take 1d20. Target can make a fort save against the push, goes against users 1d20+will mod | once per day, standard. push target 10 ft the direction they are facing. once per day. If target is pushed into another creature, they deal 1d20 damage. to each other. If target is pushed into object, they take 1d20. Target can make a fort save against the push, goes against users 1d20+will mod

items.name: Stem-Stone
items.note: Pebble that transforms into whatever inorganic substance it last touched. Currently the leather of your pouch.

items.name: Storm Giant Coins
items.note: Thirty storm giant minted gold coins, each is the size of a dinner plate and weighs 17 lbs. The image stuck on the coins shows a giant hand flicking a human off a table.

items.name: Storm Giant Horn
items.note: A horn fashioned from the translucent bone of a storm giant. Those who put their ear to the horn hear it whisper the sounds of an unknown village square. Whether the village which they hear is – or was – a real place is a mystery which may never be solved. (Twin horn in village square allows communication between the two).

items.name: Susej Cup
items.note: Turns any liquid that is poured into this cup into Elven wine.

items.name: Sword of Battle Fury
items.note: Battle Fury-grants the feat Cleave

items.name: Sword of Frost
items.note: 1d6 slashing damage, 1d6 chill damage. Freezes/Slows enemies hit, upon death enemy turns to ice and shatters, damaging all nearby enemies for 1d4 chill damage. No effect on fire or ice enemies

items.name: Talking Toad
items.note: Enchanted talking toad – polymorphed noble – knowledge of multiple regions, religions & other worldly strangeness

items.name: Tapestry
items.note: A large, well-made tapestry depicting a local duke. (Source: DMG)

items.name: The Bloody Burden
items.note: Each day this blade draws no blood it becomes heavier to bear. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: The Elven Mirror
items.note: A plain mirror with a frame of pale ashwood. Whenever someone looks in the mirror, however, they perceive an elven face in place of their own.

items.name: The Erian Tapestry
items.note: A famous (or forgotten) tapestry depicting the Fall of Erian. The figures upon the tapestry move and change, re-enacting – in an artistically abstracted form – the legendary battle over the course of half an hour.

items.name: The Guardsman's Friend
items.note:
A simple dagger with what seems to be a blood stain on the blade that cannot be washed away. On very close examination, if the leather wrapping of the handle is peeled back the phrase "All Knights are Bad" has been written on the tang. | 1d4 Piercing
Finesse - Light - Thrown: 20/60 
As a free action you can put this dagger into another creature's inventory without them noticing. (Source: OneManBland)

items.name: The Headless Helm
items.note: A frog-mouth helmet with a distinctly copper hue, though it a composite metal of some kind. The visor has a jagged curve and brings to mind the wicked smile of a Jack O'Lantern. | While wearing this helm, your head turns invisible. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: The Living Lantern
items.note:
An an ordinary candle lamp with a glass surround and handle. | When used the candle on this lantern does not burn down and the luminosity of the lantern is far beyond what a simple hooded lantern should be capable of. Once lit, the lantern slowly grows in light level until it is dangerous to look at directly. It also slowly comes to consciousness, as if being roused from a deep sleep. After an hour, the lantern is fully sapient and can parlay with the party. It has a deep desire to burn everything flammable (and can do so), but may be tempered with the promise of being kept alight for longer.

For the first 30 minutes it provides light at a range of 60/90 ft. This grows by 30 ft for every 30 minutes after. If left on for more than 3 hours the lantern becomes so hot it will set fire to anything flammable in a 5 foot range. It can be extinguished with a command word written on the base of the lantern. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: The Plaster Sword
items.note: The legendary sword once owned by the Goblin hero "Stink", who was also known as the "Hero of Thyme" after he slew a greedy Human merchant and stole a wagon of expensive cooking spices. The sword itself appears to be ornate and magical in nature from afar, but when handled it is clear that it is simply a clever replica made out of gypsum and mud that has been painted. | Due to it's storied history, the sword provides a +2 on any charisma based check when dealing with Goblins. If any attempt to use the sword as a weapon is made, it will break and can not be repaired. (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: The Rhinoclean Helm
items.note: A large iron cap with visor shaped like the head of a rhinoceros beetle. | The helm's wearer has advantage on bull rushes and can stand up from being prone once on each of its turns without expending movement. (Source: Scribnibbler21)

items.name: The Scalpel
items.note:
A surgeons scalpel, tempered so that it is both stronger and sharper than a normal blade. | 1d4+1 Piercing
finesse, light, thrown 20/60 (Source: Uberbeard)

items.name: The Torturer's Dagger
items.note:
Found on the corpse of an enemy from the realm of chaos. | 1d4 Piercing - Finesse, Light - Range: 20/60 
Any wounds inflicted using this dagger are healed two rounds later. (Source: Unknown)

items.name: Thirsty Cup
items.note: All liquid poured into this cup drains from it as if there was a hole in the bottom.

items.name: Throwing Axe of Appendage Severing
items.note: If the user of this throwing axe manages a critical hit, it will sever an appendage. – critical = appendage severed

items.name: Thunder Pommel
items.note: When embedded in the hilt of a weapon. -deal and additional 1d6 lightning damage on attacks with an odd 1d20 roll

items.name: Tiny Phantasm
items.note: A floating mass of swirling colours the size of a penny. It follows your every command and leaves a multicolour trail across any surface it touches. Cannot physically interact in any other way.

items.name: Totem Pole
items.note: A pinewood totem pole of the northern barbarians, bearing six faces (from top to bottom): a hawk, a demon, an elk, a man, a fox, and a white dragon. The hawk has a silver feather on the left side of its face (8gp), the demon has a gold nose-ring (6gp), the elk has copper antlers (4gp), the man has electrum eyes (3gp each), the fox has a black onyx nose (15gp), and the dragon has ivory teeth (30 teeth worth 1gp each). The pole is fifteen feet tall and weighs 200 pounds. (4.57m, 90.7kg)

items.name: Tourmaline Gemstone
items.note: A multi colored rectangular Tourmaline gemstone. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Townshend's Cudgel
items.note:
Townshend's Crudgels was invented by its namesake, Peter "Unknown" Townshend, a traveling bard who wished his lute to both give beauty and take it away (preferably with a good old fashioned thrashing). His lute design, later copied by some of his admirers, was a simple oak lute, reinforced with an iron shell, to maintain the soft vibration of the sound against the wooden interior, and to give the lute the strength to crack open an orc's skull with a well placed blow.
This weapon functions both as a lute and a weapon. When used as a lute, it is considered to be a lute and does not require a separate proficiency bonus to play. However, if the user rolls a critical miss with the weapon (a 1 on an attack roll) the lute may become slightly damaged and will require the user to spend an hour to retune it. If the instrument is not returned, whenever the instrument is played, the user is disadvantaged on the Perform check. This weapon can also be used as a bard's spellcasting focus. | 1d6 Bludgeoning - Versatile (1d8) (Source: D&D Wiki)

items.name: Trap Detection Key
items.note: A token/key designed to deactivate all traps within the dungeon, requires activation at security box in secret depths of deepest part of dungeon

items.name: Triple Barreled Crossbow
items.note: Triple ‘barreled’ crossbow

items.name: Troglodyte Egg
items.note: A brown and red flecked egg, about the size of a loaf of bread, that gives off a rancid scent.

items.name: Troll-Rubber Boots
items.note: Troll-rubber boots. | They give a +1 to stealth movement. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Underworld Currency
items.note: Underworld Currency – lead coins; purple rocks/crystals, fairy dust, dragon scales, dna globules (rocks/capsules with runic understanding of specific creatures), tally bones, soul traps/crystals

items.name: Visikus
items.note: This dagger belonged to the great druid, Marcek, a wise and cunning elf who fell only to the fiery Wyrm, Brazzemel, during his quest to reclaim the lost Eladrin spell tomes. Marcek inherited this dagger from his master and mentor, Garbasen the Sage, who crafted it with ancient Eladrin magic. Covered in etched runes and jewels, this dagger holds a legendary history and a powerful magic core. | 1d4 Piercing - Finesse, Light - Range: 20/60

items.name: Wand of Eye-Poking
items.note: A wand of eye poking, four charges. It will unerringly poke out the eye of a target creature within arm’s reach.

items.name: War Scythe
items.note:
Fashioned to resemble the threshing implement but modified for battle, the war scythe can be a deadly weapon in the right hands. | 1d10 Slashing
Two-Handed

You can’t wield a war scythe in one hand. If you have the Exotic Weapon Master feat, you can wield the war scythe as a war pick. It gains the versatile (d10) property when wielded in this way. When you take the Attack action, you can attempt the Trip Attack combat manuever (DC 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier) against a creature as one of your attacks. (Source: PHB)

items.name: Water Opal
items.note: Water Opal is a colourless, clear opal with a play of colour. It is rare and valuable in the Realms, where it is used in scrying devices. (Source: BGII)

items.name: Waterskin of Inexplicable Amelioration
items.note: A sheep's bladder waterskin. It is lined with a suedelike fabric which has been beautifully stiched with the image of a stag's head. | When any liquid is poured into this waterskin it is instantly purified of disease, poison or other potentially harmful contaminents.

items.name: Yggdrasil Leaf
items.note: A leaf from the great tree Yggdrasil.

items.name: Zulabar's Fist-Stick
items.note: A trolls hand mounted on a three foot rod. It will grasp objects or make a fist. Commands: Grabit, Leggo, Fist.

items.name: 1d20 Ammunition +2
items.note: You have a bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this piece of magic ammunition. The bonus is determined by the rarity of the ammunition. Once it hit a target, the ammunition is no longer magical. Value listed is for individual units. (Source: DMG)

items.name: 1d4 + 4 Beads of Force
items.note: This small black sphere measures 3/4 of an inch in diameter and weighs an ounce. You can use an action to throw the bead up to 60 feet. The bead explodes on impact and is destroyed. Each creature within a 10-foot radius of where the bead landed must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or take 5d4 force damage. A sphere of transparent force then encloses the area for 1 minute. Any creature that failed the save and is completely within the area is trapped inside this sphere. Creatures that succeeded on the save, or are partially within the area, are pushed away from the center of the sphere until they are no longer inside it. Only breathable air can pass through the sphere's wall. No attack or other effect can. An enclosed creature can use its action to push against the sphere's wall, moving the sphere up to half the creature's walking speed. The sphere can be picked up, and it's magic causes it to weigh only 1 pound, regardless of the weight of creatures inside. (Source: DMG)

items.name: A Study of Orcs, by Darius Woodherd
items.note: A large tome bound in thick bison-hide that is a common-orcish language primer, as well as a primer on orcish culture. The author of the primer, Darius Woodherd, seems to have added a lot of information on orcish heraldry and politics, as well. Darius spent almost twenty years amongst the orcs of the north, and eventually married an orc before being killed a score of years ago in a rival tribe’s ambush. | Grants a +2 circumstance check on speaking orcish, knowledge of orcish history and heraldry, and on diplomacy checks with orcs. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Adamant Leaf
items.note: A large, nearly unbreakable leaf from Tree of Adamant. | The leaf is extremely durable; no amount of force applied to the leaf is able to break it and it can only be damaged by Piercing or Slashing with a weapon. (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Adjatha the Drinker
items.note:
This blade belonged to Dabbar, a long dead servant of Bhaal that exercised control over his minions in the most brutal of ways. In addition to strengthening the mind against the guile of others, the sword absorbs life energy from an opponent with each successful hit, healing the user. Dabbar considered it a failure of his officers if he returned from battle in less than perfect health, and as the rest of the company watched, he would administer beatings until fully healed. | 1d8+2 Slashing - Versatile (1d10+2)
Heals the wielder 1 hit point every time it hits the target. Makes wielder immune to charm and domination status effects. (Source: Baldur's Gate II)

items.name: Albruin
items.note:
Basalin was a large man, rumored, far from his hearing, to be a werebear. He long wielded the bastard sword Albruin in the protection of his village, slaying giant spiders that plagued the area. The sword protected him from their poison and also allowed him to see invisible Red Wizards who controlled some of the more fantastic variants. The reason for their enmity is unknown. | 2d4+3 Slashing - Versatile (2d6+2)
Renders user immune to poison.
Can detect invisibility once per day. (Source: Baldur's Gate II)

items.name: Alexandrite Emerald
items.note: A transparent dark green Alexandrite emerald. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Algernon's Cloak
items.note: Algernon's cloak is imbued with magical properties such that the wearer will seem to shine with an inner glow. | Provides +2 to Charisma (Source: Baldur's Gate II)

items.name: Amulet of Health
items.note: This amulet is a golden disk on a chain. It usually bears the image of a lion or other powerful animal. | Your Constitution score is 19 while you wear this amulet. It has no effect on you if your Constitution is already 19 or higher. (Source: DMG)

items.name: Ancient Temple Replica
items.note: Miniature golden replica of ancient mythical temple – location map inside (Source: PlatypusPal)

items.name: Animated Pet Rock
